source_pr.title: stmtsummary: issue left in #32156
source_pr.body:

### What problem does this PR solve?

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #32156

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

### Release note


symptoms: r := tk.MustQuery("select FIRST_SEEN, LAST_SEEN from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATEMENTS_SUMMARY_HISTORY order by LAST_SEEN limit 1;") (infoschema/cluster_tables_test.go)
failure_signature: r := tk.MustQuery("select FIRST_SEEN, LAST_SEEN from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATEMENTS_SUMMARY_HISTORY order by LAST_SEEN limit 1;") (infoschema/cluster_tables_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: Statement-summary timestamps (`FIRST_SEEN`/`LAST_SEEN`) were produced using local/server timezone handling and were not consistently converted to the requested/session timezone. This made the results sensitive to environment timezone settings and could lead to unstable behavior when tests changed `time_zone`. This PR stabilizes the behavior by making the reader timezone-aware (converting timestamps into the reader/session timezone) and using a stable reference timezone (UTC) in tests.
fix_pattern: Make statement-summary timestamp rendering timezone-aware (convert `firstSeen/lastSeen` into the reader/session timezone) and avoid relying on `time.Local` by using a stable reference timezone (e.g., UTC) in tests.

evidence: evidence: r := tk.MustQuery("select FIRST_SEEN, LAST_SEEN from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATEMENTS_SUMMARY_HISTORY order by LAST_SEEN limit 1;") (infoschema/cluster_tables_test.go)
